name: Law Offices of Walter Metzen & Associates
description_short: Board-certified bankruptcy attorney in Detroit offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing services with 30+ years of experience and 20,000+ cases handled.
description_long:
Walter A. Metzen established his bankruptcy law practice in Detroit, Michigan over 30 years ago and has since become a Board Certified Specialist in Consumer Bankruptcy. The firm operates two physical office locations—one in Detroit and one in Royal Oak—and maintains an active practice serving individuals and married couples facing financial distress throughout Michigan. The practice has handled over 20,000 bankruptcy filings and helped clients eliminate hundreds of millions of dollars in consumer debt.

The firm specializes exclusively in personal bankruptcy representation, focusing on Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcies and Chapter 13 reorganization plans. They guide clients through selecting the appropriate bankruptcy chapter based on individual circumstances, including asset protection strategies and debt elimination goals. Services include credit counseling briefings, bankruptcy-specific credit reports, judgment searches, mandatory debtor education courses, and representation through the bankruptcy discharge process. The firm also offers evening appointments and conducts hearings via Zoom to increase accessibility.

Metzen differentiates his practice by emphasizing direct attorney contact rather than delegation to paralegals or legal assistants, claiming that clients meet with experienced bankruptcy counsel who has appeared in bankruptcy court thousands of times. The firm advertises transparent, all-inclusive fee structures with no hidden costs, explicitly stating that credit counseling, credit reports, judgment searches, and debtor education courses are included in quoted fees. The website emphasizes the psychological relief clients experience during initial consultations and post-discharge, positioning the service as a fresh start solution.

The practice is legitimate and well-established, with verifiable credentials and substantial case history. However, potential clients should note that bankruptcy is a serious legal process with lasting credit implications, and the choice between Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 requires thorough financial analysis. While the firm claims to evaluate whether bankruptcy is the best solution for individual circumstances, the business model inherently incentivizes bankruptcy filing over alternative debt solutions.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Board Certified Michigan Consumer Bankruptcy Specialist with recognized credentials
30+ years of bankruptcy practice experience with 20,000+ cases handled
All-inclusive fee structure explicitly states no hidden fees; includes counseling, credit reports, searches, and debtor education
Direct attorney representation—clients meet with experienced bankruptcy lawyer, not paralegals or legal assistants
Two physical office locations (Detroit and Royal Oak) plus Zoom hearing options for accessibility
Evening appointments available to accommodate working clients
Substantial debt elimination track record ($hundreds of millions discharged across client base)
cons: Bankruptcy carries severe, long-term credit consequences (7-10 years on credit report); firm emphasizes relief but not full credit impact
Business model creates inherent incentive to recommend bankruptcy even when alternatives might suffice
Website lacks transparent information on Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 outcomes and success rates for comparable cases
No pricing information published; clients must call for fee quotes, preventing price comparison
Limited discussion of asset protection specifics or property exemption details that directly impact client outcomes
best_for: Individuals with significant unsecured debt facing wage garnishment, collection harassment, or multiple creditor lawsuits
Michigan residents in financial distress who can afford attorney fees and qualify for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 relief
Homeowners or vehicle owners facing foreclosure or repossession seeking immediate bankruptcy stay protection
Married couples with joint debts seeking coordinated bankruptcy strategy and fresh financial restart
diagnosis: Best for Michigan residents facing serious financial distress—wage garnishment, collection lawsuits, foreclosure, or repossession—who have exhausted other options and can afford attorney representation. The main caveat is that bankruptcy is a severe legal remedy with 7-10 year credit consequences; this firm should be viewed as a last-resort solution provider rather than a first-line debt advisor, and clients should independently verify that bankruptcy is superior to debt consolidation, settlement, or management alternatives.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and representation (liquidation bankruptcy)
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and representation (reorganization/payment plan bankruptcy)
Bankruptcy chapter selection and financial situation evaluation
Asset protection and property exemption planning
Bankruptcy-specific credit report analysis (included in fees)
Public records search for judgments on credit report (included in fees)
Mandatory debtor education course completion (included in fees)
Bankruptcy discharge petition and court representation
Creditor harassment prevention and collection call defense
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
